title: autobench.iteration.summary v1
description: Rollup emitted at completion of each RSI iteration. Consumers (pulse dashboard, continuous.py surprise digest, deer-flow analytics) previously had to recompute aggregate_score, pass_rate, verdict_distribution and cost from the raw autobench.case.result.v1 stream — this channel publishes the rollup that the server already computed in evaluator.BenchmarkResult so downstream consumers don't have to. Source: /autobench.

session_id.description: ULID for the autobench run; correlates with case.result events

iteration.description: RSI iteration number this summary describes

total_latency_ms.description: Sum of per-case wall-clock latency in milliseconds

total_cost_usd.description: Sum of worker-call USD cost for this iteration. 0.0 when worker-call cost rollup is not threaded through (see nervous-bus follow-up for cost plumbing).

total_tokens.description: Sum of prompt+completion tokens across worker calls in this iteration. 0 when worker-call token rollup is not threaded through.

verdict_distribution.description: Histogram mapping verdict string (OK/WA/TLE/RE/CE/MLE/VF/RV/RD/RT) to count

num_cases.description: Number of benchmark cases evaluated this iteration

harness_version.description: Harness version tag for the iteration (e.g. 'v3')

ce_rate.description: Fraction of cases with verdict==CE (compilation error)

ok_rate.description: Fraction of cases with verdict==OK (synonym for pass_rate; included for explicit access from consumers that key on verdict rates)
